lesson.menu_title: Who Is Lucia Serra in English? -- Mapping the Gap
lesson.menu_desc: Diagnostic + baseline: your career, your goals, your voice in English -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 1 -- Who Is Lucia Serra in English?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Lucia Nishiyama Serra | Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic + Baseline

listenings.text: I started my career almost thirty years ago as a junior developer, writing code all day. Over the years, I moved into management, and then I led large engineering teams in three countries. Honestly, I never planned any of it. These days my work looks completely different. Right now I am building a new team that focuses on the safety of artificial intelligence, and it is the most interesting challenge of my whole career. I am learning something new every single week. People often ask me for the secret. There is no secret. I show up, I stay curious, and I am not afraid to say when I do not understand something.

I help senior leaders prepare presentations in English, and I always tell them the same thing. A great presentation is not about perfect grammar. It is about connection. Every week I work with executives who speak beautifully in their own language but freeze in English. So we practice out loud, again and again, until the words feel natural. This month I am coaching three leaders who are getting ready for interviews in the United States. They are nervous, of course. But confidence is a skill, not a gift. You build it slowly, one conversation at a time. My advice is simple. Speak, make mistakes, and keep going.

extra_audio.text: First, Lucia says her name and where she is from. Then she talks about her long career in technology. Next, she says what she does today, in AI security. After that, she explains what she is focusing on these days. Finally, she says her goal: interviews and presentations in the United States.
